title: Leftover Roasted Root Soup With Stuffing Croutons
summary: Make the most of your leftovers carrots, parsnips, onions, and potatoes by making a hearty soup. The stuffing croutons are so good you'll want them all year round.

ingredients.label: 600g Leftover Roasted Root Vegetables (We Used Carrots, Parsnips, Onions, Potatoes And A Bulb Of Garlic)
ingredients.name: Roasted Vegetables

ingredients.label: 100ml Double Cream, Plus Extra For Drizzling
ingredients.name: Double Cream

method.description: Heat the oven to 200°C. Add water to the stuffing following pack instructions, then spread the stuffing in a thin layer over the base of a large, lightly greased baking tray. Rough-up with a fork and pop in the oven for 20 mins. After it’s cooked, break the stuffing up and toss in a little oil, then pop back in the oven for 20-30 mins until crispy – keep checking on it and giving it a mix, so it doesn't burn. Meanwhile, finely chop your parsley.

Set a large pot over a medium heat and add the stock. Roughly chop up the vegetables and add them to the stock, then simmer for 10 mins. Cool slightly, then blend with a stick blender until smooth. Mix through the cream. Taste and season with salt and pepper. Once the stuffing is crisp, pour the soup into bowls and top with a drizzle of cream, crispy stuffing, Parmesan and some parsley.
